title: Boat Rental in Berlin Spree: How to Choose the Perfect Vessel for 2026
html:
Navigating Berlin Spree demands a vessel suited to city canals and wider lake stretches. Renting a boat from Treptow harbour costs from €120 for a small motorboat half-day to €1,500 for a crewed yacht, and platforms like BoatHire24 help you compare options.

Where to set off in Berlin Spree?

The best departure points include Treptower Park pier, Friedrichshagen Marina, Spandau Yachthafen, and the historic Rummelsburger Bucht. Treptower Park offers easy access to the central Spree and Landwehrkanal. Friedrichshafen Marina is a large facility with fuel and pump-out. Spandau Yachthafen sits at the confluence of Spree and Havel, while Rummelsburger Bucht provides a wide, sheltered bay ideal for beginners. Further out, Müggelsee lake has a public pier in Friedrichshagen village. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent in Berlin Spree?

Your choice depends on group size, experience, and how far you aim to cruise. Here is a ranked list of popular types.

  1. Day-cruiser motorboat, best for small groups of 4, 6 exploring central canals, from €120 half-day.
  2. Pontoon boat, ideal for relaxed parties of up to 10, with sun deck and BBQ facilities, from €250 half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 40), for experienced sailors on the Havel lakes, 6 guests, from €450 full-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Princess V58), for premium charter with 8 guests and full crew, from €1,500 per day.
  5. Electric or solar boat, quiet and eco-friendly, perfect for 4, 5 on the Spree, from €100 for two hours.

When comparing motor yachts, consider that this motor yacht, the Princess V58, offers 17 metres of comfort with twin Volvo Penta engines. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Berlin Spree?

Prices range from €50 per person for a shared tour to €2,000 for a crewed luxury yacht day.

  • Shared one-hour tour boat: €15, 25 per person (no licence).
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €120, €200.
  • Pontoon boat full-day (8 hours): €350, €600.
  • Catamaran full-day (Lagoon 42, 10 guests): €700, €1,200.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (8 guests including skipper): €1,200, €2,000.

To secure availability for summer 2026, consider booking a charter online. You can also see more at browse the listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Berlin Spree?

A German Sportbootführerschein is required for motorboats over 15 hp inland, but some rentals provide skippers or offer smaller boats without licence. The local authority enforces the inland waterways licence from April to October. Many charter companies include a qualified skipper, so you can relax without a licence. Always check with the operator, they confirm exact requirements. For unlicenced hire, low-horsepower electric boats are available.

Tip: Book a skipper for your first trip on the Spree; they can navigate the locks at Mühlendamm and share history of Berlin’s riverbanks.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €2,000 per day
  • Peak months: May, September (high season July, August)
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (typical half-day 4 hours)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence needed: Yes for over 15 hp; skipper option

Questions about boat rental in Berlin Spree

Can I return the boat to a different marina?

Most rental companies require return to the original harbour, but a few offer one-way trips on the Havel with a fee and prior arrangement.

What is the best time of day to cruise the Spree?

Early morning (before 10am) offers calm waters and fewer tourist boats; late afternoon (4, 7pm) pairs with sunset views over the Reichstag.

Are there restaurants accessible only by boat in Berlin?

Yes, several gastronomic spots have private moorings along the Spree, such as the Badeschiff beach bar and the historic Schleusenkrug beer garden.

Do I need to book in advance for a day trip in summer 2026?

Booking at least one month ahead is highly recommended for weekend dates and popular motor yacht types during July and August.

For 2026, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Berlin Spree or list your own. To browse current availability and owner profiles, you can browse the listings on the homepage. Boat owners are also invited to list their vessel and earn income from charter.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Berlin Spree and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
